strengths: Rigorous application of the CARDS framework to evaluate a key writing source, demonstrating strong research and critical thinking skills
Consistent self-management evidenced by a structured weekly writing plan and completion of 80 pages ahead of schedule
Effective use of thinking skills shown in a mind map that clarifies complex character relationships and deepens narrative design
weaknesses: Limited expert feedbackâconsider engaging with online writing communities or peer forums to enrich your communication and obtain diverse perspectives
feedback.calculationMethod:
Mini report holistic evaluation: **You have leveraged a range of ATL skills effectively.** Your use of the CARDS framework highlights thorough research practices, while the disciplined writing schedule reflects excellent self-management. The mind map illustrates clear, strategic thinking applied to story development. 

**To bolster your project impact**, actively seek out expert feedback. Tapping into writing groups or forums will broaden your input sources and strengthen both the process and final product.

strengths: Clear articulation of a learning goal tightly connected to personal interests in languages and storytelling
Well-defined intended product (a 150-page Spanish mystery novel for young adults) that focuses the project and its audience
Detailed sequencing of long- and short-term goals with dates and completion notes, demonstrating a feasible, structured plan
weaknesses: One of the success criteria (Content) is truncated and lacks full articulation, which may lead to ambiguity in evaluation
feedback.calculationMethod:
Mini report holistic evaluation: **The planning section excels in establishing strong motivation and a concrete target.** Your learning goal is **authentically linked** to your interests, and the choice of a 150-page Spanish mystery novel gives clear direction. The inclusion of both long- and short-term milestones, complete with dates and progress notes, shows a **well-sequenced and realistic roadmap** for completion. 

**To refine this further**, ensure that every success criterion is fully described. The truncated âContentâ criterion should be expanded so that it is as specific and measurable as the others, preventing any uncertainty when assessing your final product.

strengths: Use of handwritten conclusions (Fig 10) as concrete evidence, reinforcing reflective insights on research outcomes
Insightful reflection on how research skills evolved and directly influenced writing practice
Integration of reader-feedback data (Fig 12) to reassess and refine success criteria, demonstrating stakeholder-informed evaluation
Comprehensive synthesis of product and learning goals in the overall evaluation, showing strong self-assessment and justification
weaknesses: The role of the follow-up questionnaire in refining chapter structure is unclearâclarify how its findings led to your final organisational decisions
feedback.calculationMethod:
Mini report holistic evaluation: **The reflective component is particularly robust.** You support your reflections with tangible artifactsâhandwritten notes and feedback figuresâand show **deep self-awareness** by tracing your growth in research skills to tangible improvements in writing. Your incorporation of reader-feedback illustrates a **mature, stakeholder-driven** approach to evaluation, and your final synthesis effectively ties together both learning and product objectives. 

**For even greater clarity**, explain precisely how the follow-up questionnaire data influenced your judgments about chapter organisation. This will resolve any gaps between your initial criteria and the final outcome, strengthening the coherence of your reflection.

annotations.comment: You note difficulty securing expert feedback; consider leveraging online writing communities or peer forums to broaden your communication and obtain more diverse input.
You articulate a clear learning goal linked to personal interests in languages and storytelling, demonstrating authentic motivation and a strong foundation for your projectâs planning.
Your success criteria are mostly specific and measurable, but the description for âContentâ is truncated. Ensure every criterion is fully articulated to avoid ambiguity.
The detailed long- and short-term goals, complete with dates and completion notes, demonstrate a feasible, sequenced plan that supports on-schedule progress.
Excellent use of the CARDS framework to evaluate your key writing source, showing rigorous research skills and critical assessment of credibility and accuracy.
Including handwritten conclusions (Fig 10) as evidence reinforces your reflection on research outcomes and shows meaningful integration of learning into practice.
Strong self-management is evident in your structured weekly plan and consistent writing habit, resulting in 80 pages completed ahead of schedule.
Your mind map clearly visualizes complex character relationships, showcasing effective application of thinking skills to organize and deepen your narrative design.
You meet the Aesthetics criteria with clear, objective evidence (A5 format, paper density), showing precise alignment of your product with physical success measures.
You provide insightful reflection on how your research skills evolved and directly impacted your writing, demonstrating strong self-awareness of learning growth.
When evaluating Organisation, clarify how your follow-up questionnaire influenced your final judgement on chapter structure to resolve the discrepancy between initial criteria and outcomes.
Your use of reader-feedback data (Fig 12) to reassess success criteria demonstrates a robust reflective approach by integrating stakeholder input into your evaluation.
Your overall evaluation synthesizes both learning and product goals effectively, demonstrating comprehensive self-assessment and justification of met criteria.
You define your intended product clearlyâa 150-page Spanish mystery novel for young adultsâwhich provides a focused, audience-driven target for your project.
